name: Rotelle with Smoked Turkey, Broccoli and Peppers
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Smoked turkey (or chicken), skin removed, diced
1/3 cup Jar-packed roasted red peppers, drained, diced
1/2 cup Chicken stock or canned broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook broccoli in a large pot of boiling salted water until crisp-tender, about 2 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer broccoli to a bowl of ice water to cool; reserve the cooking liquid in the pot. Drain the broccoli and pat dry. Return the reserved cooking liquid to boil.
2. Add pasta to the boiling water and cook until just tender but still firm to the bite, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking. Drain thoroughly.
3. Meanwhile, melt butter with olive oil in a heavy large skillet over low heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, and sauté until garlic is tender, about 3 minutes.
4. Add turkey to the skillet and sauté until heated through, about 4 minutes.
5. Mix broccoli, pasta, roasted peppers, and stock into the turkey mixture. Increase heat to high and cook until the pasta has absorbed most of the liquid, stirring frequently, about 4 minutes.
6. Divide the pasta among plates and serve, passing Romano cheese separately.
